title: 以下、Kernel/FUGUEに活用できる？ Server: SimpleHTTP/0.6 Python/3.11.9
summary_text: **Concise Findings (Governance)** - **Policy inheritance risk (High):** Codex loads skills from `REPO`, `USER`, `ADMIN`, and `SYSTEM` locations, scans upward to repo root, and does **not** merge duplicate skill names. **Implication (inference):** same task can resolve to different skills across hosts/users; naming collisions create policy ambiguity. - **Auditability split (Medium):** Repo-scoped skills are auditable via Git; user/admin/system-scoped skills are harder to govern centrally. **Implication (inference):** incomplete forensic trail unless runtime captures exact skill path/version. - **Version drift / supply-chain risk (High):** Skills can be installed from names/folders/URLs via `$skill-installer`; system skills are bundled by Codex. **Implication (inference):** behavior can change outside your release process unless pinned. - **Reproducibility risk (High):** Skill activation can be implicit from `description`; `allow_implicit_invocation` defaults to `true`. **Implication (inference):** non-deterministic lane behavior unless explicit invocation is enforced. - **Reviewability gaps (Medium):** Skills may include `scripts/`, `references/`, and `agents/openai.yaml` (tool dependencies, policy). **Implication (inference):** governance misses happen if review focuses only on `SKILL.md`. - **Trust-boundary risk (Medium):** Codex follows symlinked skill folders. **Implication (inference):** path-based controls can be bypassed if symlink targets are not constrained. **Guardrails for Kernel/FUGUE** - Enforce **repo-pinned skills only** for orchestrated runs; block `USER/ADMIN` skills in production lanes. - Require org-unique naming (`<org>-<domain>-<skill>`) and fail on collisions. - For privileged/high-impact skills, set `policy.allow_implicit_invocation: false`; require explicit `$skill` in lane prompts. - Record lane evidence: `skill_name`, absolute `skill_path`, Git commit SHA (or content hash), invocation mode (`explicit|implicit`), and tool dependencies used. - Pin installs to approved commits/internal mirrors; disallow floating branch URLs in governed environments. - Add mandatory review gates on `SKILL.md`, `scripts/**`, and `agents/openai.yaml` (CODEOWNERS + security checklist). - Deny or strictly allowlist symlink targets for skill directories. Sources: - https://developers.openai.com/codex/skills - https://github.com/openai/skills
